title: Implementing Khan Academy with students
description: A check for understanding of the Implementing Khan Academy with students lesson.
topic_path: Supporting student progress with Khan Academy
Support student progress with Khan Academy

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following is the recommended way to implement Khan Academy with students?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: The recommended way to implement Khan Academy is to **use targeted assignments to strategically support student Mastery goals**.

choices.content: Use targeted assignments to strategically support student Mastery goals.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Fill in the blanks.**

By pairing [[☃ dropdown 1]] with [[☃ dropdown 5]], you combine the best pieces of long-term and short-term [[☃ dropdown 3]] work to accelerate student  [[☃ dropdown 4]]  learning.
hints: By pairing **Course Mastery goals** with **Unit Mastery goals**, you combine the best pieces of long-term and short-term **student-paced** work to accelerate student  **skill** learning.

assessment_items.question_content:
**When setting up your classroom for Khan Academy use, which of the following are recommended?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: When using Khan Academy in the classroom teacher recommend **headphones for students**, **whiteboards or scratch paper** for student work, space for student collaboration and student-driven goal-tracking.

choices.content: Space for students to work independently only

assessment_items.question_content:
**If you're new to using Khan Academy, which strategy is recommended as the simplest for initial use with students?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Many teachers recommend having the **whole class** work on the same topic as a great starting point for those new to Khan Academy.

assessment_items.question_content:
**If you're using Khan Academy with students, which strategy is recommended if you have a limited number of devices?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: **Station rotation** allows students to work on necessary content, keeps all students engaged, and reduces the need for all students to have a device at all times.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Fill in the blanks.**

One piece of advice frequently given by teachers is to practice using Khan Academy as a [[☃ dropdown 1]]. Choose a topic that interests you - the [[☃ dropdown 3]] you teach is a great starting point - and [[☃ dropdown 2]] it on Khan Academy!
hints: One piece of advice frequently given by teachers is to practice using Khan Academy as a **student**. Choose a topic that interests you - the **course** you teach is a great starting point - and **study** it on Khan Academy!

assessment_items.question_content:
**What is a common suggestion for students, other than asking their teacher for help, for when they get stuck on Khan Academy? **

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: If students are stuck on a problem, they can **watch a video**, **try a hint**, or **ask a classmate**.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Fill in the blanks.**

Use Khan Academy's [[☃ dropdown 1]] report to identify students who are at the same [[☃ dropdown 2]] level for a given skill. This is a great opportunity to create an [[☃ dropdown 3]] in which all students receive the same set of questions in order to encourage collaboration.
hints: Use Khan Academy's **skills** report to identify students who are at the same **mastery** level for a given skill. This is a great opportunity to create an **assignment** in which all students receive the same set of questions in order to encourage collaboration.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Fill in the blanks.**

A [[☃ dropdown 1]]  mastery goal is set to evaluate student progress toward understanding the entirety of the course content. On the other hand, [[☃ dropdown 2]]  mastery goals focus on individual sections of the course, allowing teachers to determine which students have grasped a specific "chunk" of the course material and who might need further support.
hints: A **course** mastery goal is set to evaluate student progress toward understanding the entirety of the course content. On the other hand, **unit** mastery goals focus on individual sections of the course, allowing teachers to determine which students have grasped a specific "chunk" of the course material and who might need further support.

assessment_items.question_content:
By tracking student performance and progress, teachers can identify gaps in understanding and adjust the assignments accordingly.

**True or false.**

[[☃ dropdown 1]]
hints: True. By tracking student performance and progress, teachers can identify gaps in understanding and adjust the assignments accordingly.
